summary: McKenzie discovers that his Google AdWords optimization has been so effective at minimizing cost-per-click that his daily budget can now cover all available clicks, leaving money unspent. He shares detailed metrics for two ad groups promoting Bingo Card Creator, with strong click-through and conversion rates. He shifts strategy from minimizing CPC to maximizing total conversions, and considers expanding to international English-speaking markets.
key_points: Over-optimizing for low CPC can leave budget unspent when you've exhausted available impressions at that price point
His Dolch word list ad group generates 3.5x more impressions but converts at a lower rate than the direct bingo card group
Both ad groups maintain cost-per-acquisition under $0.52, showing strong unit economics for a micro-ISV
The natural next step after mastering CPC is shifting focus to maximizing total conversion volume
money_quotes: It turns out that I was so successful at minimizing my CPC I am now able to pay for all the clicks my ads would generate in a day, with money left over.
Time to see if I can't increase the CTR a bit without increasing my cost per conversion that much…
That reminds me, I should start advertising in Australia/Great Britain soon.
key_insight: Optimizing for the lowest possible cost-per-click eventually hits a ceiling where the real constraint becomes total conversion volume, requiring a strategic shift in ad spending approach.
